title: PathwayBench: a multi-criterion benchmark of pseudobulk pathway activity scoring methods reveals rank-window competition as a mechanism of biological signal loss in single-cell RNA-seq
description: PathwayBench is a multi-criterion benchmark of pathway activity scoring methods for the pseudobulk case-control regime of single-cell RNA-seq. It compares five widely used methods (ssGSEA, GSVA, z-score, AUCell, UCell) across eight pseudobulked scRNA-seq case-control datasets spanning five tissues (brain, heart, kidney, lung, blood) and 682 donors (4,256,380 cells; 5,923 pseudobulk samples; 1,864 pathway-level comparisons per method across 10 Reactome pathways). Each method is evaluated on five criteria: biological relevance (direction accuracy, AUROC, effect size) and four robustness axes (aggregation, outlier, normalization, and sample-size stability), with per-criterion performance discretized as Good/Intermediate/Poor against fixed thresholds. The benchmark identifies rank-window competition, a mechanism by which rank-based methods (AUCell, UCell) lose, and in extreme cases invert, biological signal when competitor genes saturate the top-rank scoring window, demonstrated in controlled simulations and confirmed in extracellular matrix remodeling in chronic kidney disease. PathwayBench ships per-dataset scores, evaluation code, a public Streamlit advisor application, and full reproducibility infrastructure. This record (v3.0) corresponds to the eight-dataset, five-criterion analysis.
